goal: Remediate AUD-20260715-tlo6jc through the full 13-phase contract, then complete real three-journey acceptance, an independent audit, pristine restoration, and a v1.8.1 release.

limitations: Exact-ref GitHub connector reads were used, but the isolated execution environment could not clone github.com because outbound DNS was unavailable; tests and builds were not executed locally.
The reviewed HEAD has no GitHub combined-status entries or workflow runs.
No persisted exact-HEAD evidence demonstrates all three real Web Pro journeys, a fresh post-fix independent audit, pristine release state, or v1.8.1 tag binding.
The request patch bytes were not separately exposed for independent SHA-256 recomputation; GitHub compare confirmed base/head ancestry, commit count, and file roster.

P1.title: Claim ownership and cross-process fencing are not durably bound
observedEvidence: Claim and result-mutation tools carry no claimant/session token or fencing epoch; they identify authority only by requestId.
claimRequest persists only the claimed lifecycle state, not an owner record.
Any authenticated caller with the requestId can begin, upload, or finalize after another caller claimed the request.
The cross-process lease has a fixed acquiredAt and no heartbeat; a second process deletes it after the stale threshold.
assertLease and destination rename are separate operations, so the lock fingerprint is not an atomic compare-and-swap fence.
The stale-owner test replaces a transient lock during one operation and does not model two durable review sessions or an active operation exceeding the lease TTL.
P1.inference: The implementation serializes common calls but cannot prove reviewer ownership or strict stale-owner exclusion across clients and processes. A live operation can be reclaimed by time and an unrelated authorized client can continue another session's claim.
P1.impact: Result authorship and mailbox integrity remain vulnerable to credential replay, multiple clients, process stalls, slow filesystems, and long finalization operations.
requiredRemediation: Persist a versioned claim record with an unguessable claim token and monotonic fencing epoch.
Require the claim token or versioned successor authority on all result mutations.
Bind upload descriptors, journals, and revisions to the claim epoch and reject stale epochs.
Use renewable leases or atomic compare-and-swap storage; do not reclaim an active owner solely from fixed age.
Eliminate the check-then-rename fencing gap by verifying the epoch as part of the durable commit.
requiredTests: Two independent clients where only the claimant can mutate
Stale claim token after reclaim
Finalize lasting longer than lease TTL
Reclaim racing between fence check and rename
Restart preserves or explicitly reclaims claim authority
Credential replay cannot mutate a claimed request

P1.title: Mandatory real acceptance, independent audit, and release closure are absent
observedEvidence: The iteration exit contract still requires three real journeys, a fresh independent audit with no P0/P1, pristine restoration, and v1.8.1.
Sprint vpb-09 explicitly excludes phases 11 through 13.
The handoff remains active and states that real GitHub connector and MCP write confirmation is pending.
package.json remains version 0.1.0 and harnessVersion 1.7.30.
All three remediation commit messages state Verification: pending.
GitHub reports no combined statuses and no workflow runs for the reviewed HEAD.
P1.impact: The original goal cannot be marked complete or release-authorized from this snapshot, and unresolved P1 authority defects would be shipped if closure were claimed.
requiredRemediation: Resolve every P1 finding.
Execute and persist bounded evidence for the real CLI-manual, CLI-mailbox, and Web-origin design journeys.
Use actual ChatGPT Web Pro, GitHub connector grounding, and Bridge MCP write tools where required.
Run a fresh-context independent audit after implementation freeze and rerun it after any code change.
Run the full maintained verification suite and preserve exact exit codes and test counts.
Restore pristine state, update all version surfaces to a non-colliding semver, create one release commit, and bind a tag exactly to it.
Do not push without explicit active-user authorization.
requiredTests: Real CLI-origin manual journey
Real CLI-origin MCP mailbox journey
Real Web-origin design journey
Wrong-repository rejection before write
Fresh independent whole-workflow audit with P0/P1 zero
Exact-HEAD full suite and sync audits
Pristine worktree, version consistency, and tag points-at-HEAD receipt

P2.title: The advertised one-time connector code remains replayable
observedEvidence: The connector URL embeds connectCode in the code query parameter and calls it a one-time exchange code.
After first use, authorize continues accepting the same connectCode until the default 12-hour session TTL expires.
The generated sessionToken is not returned to the real client by an exchange response.
Tests deliberately reuse the same query code for initialize and tools/list and expect success.
Tests deliberately authorize eight concurrent first requests with the same code.
P2.inference: The query code is the reusable session bearer rather than a one-time bootstrap credential.
P2.impact: A capability URL leak grants mailbox tool access for the session lifetime and compounds the ownerless mutation defect.
requiredRemediation: Invalidate the bootstrap code atomically on the first successful exchange.
Return a verified client-usable short-lived credential through OAuth, Authorization configuration, or a connector-supported session mechanism.
If credential rotation is impossible, document the URL accurately as a bearer, shorten TTL, and strengthen revocation and logging controls rather than claiming one-time semantics.
requiredTests: Second use of bootstrap code is rejected
Concurrent exchange has exactly one winner
Returned session credential works and expires
Revoked credential is rejected
No reusable credential is logged

P2.title: Request-associated manual bundles bypass the unbound-result gate
observedEvidence: runBundleSync defines unbound only as request metadata being absent.
A requestId lookup or web-origin --latest can associate the manual bundle with a stored request and rewrite the bundle requestId.
Without a result manifest, the importer skips result hash, request hash, repository, reviewed-head, file roster, file SHA, and reviewer-declaration validation.
The explicit accept-unbound flag is not required once a request is found.
Skipped validations are printed after installation on this path, and out-of-band acknowledgement can mark the request imported.
Tests characterize this path as a bound Web-origin result despite absent result-manifest binding.
P2.inference: Request association is incorrectly substituted for result authority, allowing stale or substituted package bytes to be installed for a valid request.
P2.impact: A syntactically compliant but unverified manual result can become installed and acknowledged without the high-friction trust decision promised by the remediation.
requiredRemediation: Require a result manifest or exported receipt binding repository, reviewed HEAD, request hash, result hash, file roster, per-file hashes, and reviewer declaration before calling a result bound.
Treat missing result binding as unbound even if request metadata exists.
Print skipped validations before any write and require explicit acceptance.
Do not rewrite web-origin to a mailbox requestId without a bound association receipt.
Do not acknowledge an unbound package as the authoritative imported terminal state.
requiredTests: Forged bundle against --latest is rejected
Stale bundle against a newer request is rejected
Missing manifest requires explicit acceptance before write
Changed file bytes and wrong request hash are rejected
Unbound accepted package cannot count as authoritative imported release evidence

P2.title: Revision-gap filling records inverted provenance
observedEvidence: The importer selects the lowest free revision number but always chooses the highest occupied slot as predecessor.
With rev1 and rev3 present, filling rev2 records revisionOf=rev3 and rev3's result hash.
The focused test explicitly asserts that rev2 points to rev3.
P2.impact: The immutable package lineage becomes non-monotonic and can contain forward references or cycles, weakening audit and rollback integrity.
requiredRemediation: Define revision lineage semantics explicitly.
For gap filling, choose the highest occupied revision lower than the selected revision, or prohibit gap filling and append only after the highest revision.
Validate that revisionOf resolves to an existing lower revision and that predecessorResultSha256 matches it.
requiredTests: rev1 plus rev3 gap gives rev2 predecessor rev1
Multiple gaps remain acyclic
No forward revision references
Predecessor hash matches immutable provenance
Malformed legacy chains are diagnosed
